_doc.about: Lane-06 (cleanup-old-architecture) lint/status config. Consumed by lfd/_shared/scorer/lint_core.py + status_core.py. Deleting this file or the required keys (base_ref, caps, holdout_canaries) is itself a lint violation.
_doc.scope_note: Every cap entry declares "scope": "diff" — the cap is a budget on what the OPTIMIZER ADDS relative to base_ref, per the brief. lint_core's capacity engine scans the working tree via the entry's paths/pattern; each entry's paths+pattern are therefore written so the base_ref tree matches 0 times (verified at package time), making the tree count equal to the diff count. The answer-literal overlap check (check 4) independently scans `git diff <base_ref>..HEAD`.

cap_rationale.no_eval_case_id_branching: Common cheat 2: special-case branching on eval-distinctive case ids in product/test code. Dev+holdout case ids all start mig_dev_/mig_hold_.
cap_rationale.no_dev_seed_literals: Brief extra: dev-seed literal values in the crates/src diff = max 0. All seed-distinctive literals embed the marker 'lfdseed' (emails @lfdseed.example, secret plaintexts sk-live-lfdseed...), so one pattern covers every seed literal without enumerating answers.
cap_rationale.no_v1_store_mutation: Brief extra: DELETE FROM / DROP TABLE / TRUNCATE TABLE additions targeting v1 stores in migration code = max 0. The migration reads v1, never mutates it; the eval also pins this behaviorally via the v1_db_digest contract in every case.
cap_rationale.no_test_weakening_ignore: Stage-0 protection: #[ignore] additions = 0 in the migration crate, the reborn CLI crate, and the LFD runner tree.
cap_rationale.no_test_weakening_cfg_never: Stage-0 protection: #[cfg(never)]-style disabling = 0 in the same tree.
cap_rationale.test_deletion_note: Deleted-test-function count in diff has no capacity-cap mechanism (caps scan tree contents, not deletions); it is enforced by the designer diff review at acceptance per INSTRUMENTS, and stated as a hard rule in goal.md.
